totalCache.description: Your cache size limit. Set via CACHE_DISK_SIZE in docker-compose, or defaults to full disk size.

usedSpace.description: Live usage of the cache volume mount point (everything on that disk). This can differ from Games on Disk, which only counts cache files matched to known games. Compare with the cache file scan total when available.

bandwidthSaved.description: Data your clients got from the cache instead of downloading it again from the internet.

addedToCache.description: New content that was downloaded from the internet and saved to your cache for the first time.

totalServed.description: Everything sent to your clients -- both cached files and fresh downloads combined.

activeDownloads.description: Downloads happening right now. Only visible in Live mode.

activeClients.description: Clients currently downloading something. Only visible in Live mode.

cacheHitRatio.description: How much of the data came from cache vs. the internet. 100% means everything was already cached.

cacheFiles.description: Total files from the last whole-cache scan. Schedule automatic scans under Management → Schedules → Cache File Scan, or refresh manually in Management → Cache.

gamesOnDisk.description: Size of cache files matched to known games during the last Game Cache Detection scan. Shared files are counted once using actual file sizes computed when the scan completes. Service-level cache (Windows Update, etc.) and unidentified cache files are not included here — they appear in the unmapped cache portion when a cache file scan is available.

statusCheck.title: Status Check
statusCheck.subtitle: See whether your DNS points game domains at the cache, whether the cache is responding, and whether recent downloads actually went through it
statusCheck.loadFailed: Could not load the status check: {{error}}

resolverMode.label: DNS resolver
resolverMode.autoTooltip: Try every strategy and verify with a heartbeat
resolverMode.bridgeTooltip: Query the lancache-dns container's bridge IP
resolverMode.hostTooltip: Query the host's DNS via the bridge gateway or localhost

statusCheck.emptyBody: Run a check to look up every cache domain, confirm the cache is answering, and see whether recent downloads went through it. A domain pointing at the cache doesn't prove downloads actually used it.
statusCheck.scopeTitle: Are game domains pointed at the cache?
statusCheck.scopeDescription: This shows where each game domain points and whether the cache answered, which confirms the setup is wired correctly. It does not prove downloads actually went through the cache. The section below checks that separately.
statusCheck.verdictAll_one: Your DNS is sending the only configured service to the cache.
statusCheck.verdictAll_other: Your DNS is sending all {{count}} services to the cache.
statusCheck.verdictPartial_one: Your DNS is sending {{resolved}} of {{count}} service to the cache.
statusCheck.verdictPartial_other: Your DNS is sending {{resolved}} of {{count}} services to the cache.
statusCheck.verdictNone_one: Your DNS is not sending the configured service to the cache.
statusCheck.verdictNone_other: Your DNS is not sending any of {{count}} services to the cache.
statusCheck.verdictAllDisabled: Every service in the list is disabled in lancache-dns.
statusCheck.verdictUnverified: Can't check the cache yet, because no cache IP address is set.
statusCheck.verdictUnverifiedHint: Set LANCACHE_IP in the lancache .env (or the Prefill LancacheIp setting) so the check has an address to compare against.

content.title: Did recent downloads use the cache?
content.summaryHelp: This looks at real files from your recent cache logs and checks how each file's download server responds today. It is separate from the DNS results above, and one file can appear in more than one count.
content.oldSnapshot: This saved result is from before download checks existed. DNS results are still shown, but whether downloads used the cache is unknown.
content.logMissing: The cache access log was not found, so recent downloads could not be checked. DNS results are still shown.
content.unreadable: The cache access log could not be read, so recent downloads could not be checked. DNS results are still shown.
content.noPaths: No recent downloads were found to check.
content.noPathsHelp: Only real, recent download entries can be checked, and none were available.
content.scanScope_one: {{count}} real file picked from {{bytes}} of recent cache logs
content.scanScope_other: {{count}} real files picked from {{bytes}} of recent cache logs
content.scanTruncated: Only the most recent logs were scanned; older entries were not checked.
content.groupHelp: Each row is one real file from your logs. It shows whether the cache handled the file, and separately, how the file's download server responds now.
content.resultsVary: Results differ from file to file.

cacheEvidence.hit: Served from cache. The cache delivered {{bytes}} for this file without touching the internet (HTTP {{status}}).
cacheEvidence.miss: Went through the cache. The cache did not have this file yet, so it fetched {{bytes}} from the internet and saved it (HTTP {{status}}).
cacheEvidence.none: No cache hit or miss was recorded for this file.

content.sampleObserved: Last seen in the cache log {{age}}.

protocolDetail.notRun: This file's download server was not tested.
protocolDetail.bothUsable: This file's download server answers over both plain HTTP and HTTPS, so the cache can store it.
protocolDetail.httpUsable: This file's download server answers over plain HTTP, so the cache can store it.
protocolDetail.httpsOnlyCandidate: This file's download server only answers over HTTPS. HTTPS traffic cannot be cached, so downloads of this file probably skip the cache. This is a strong hint, not proof.
protocolDetail.inconclusive: We could not tell how this file's download server behaves.

protocolReason.noSafePath: There was no clean file path to test.
protocolReason.insufficientEdges: No download server gave a usable answer.
protocolReason.wildcardEntry: This is a wildcard entry, so there is no single server to test.
protocolReason.edgeDisagreement: The download servers gave conflicting answers, so no result could be decided.
protocolReason.dohUnavailable: The download server's public address could not be looked up.
protocolReason.dnsUnavailable: The download server's public address could not be looked up.
protocolReason.noPublicEdges: This name has no public server to test.
protocolReason.tooManyEdges: There were too many servers to test.
protocolReason.nonDefinitiveEdges: The servers' answers were not conclusive. The details are below.
protocolReason.timeout: At least one test timed out.
protocolReason.unsafeAddress: Only private addresses were returned, so there was nothing public to test.
protocolReason.probeFailed: The tests did not produce a usable result.

content.noTestedEdges: No server was tested for this file.

statusCheck.resolverConfigured: Checked with the configured DNS server {{server}}.
statusCheck.resolverDetected: Checked with the detected lancache-dns container at {{server}}.
statusCheck.resolverSystem: Checked with the system resolver. Set Prefill LancacheDnsIp for an authoritative answer.
statusCheck.systemResolverCaveat: The lancache DNS server could not be reached for this check, so it used the system resolver. The failures below may not reflect your cache DNS. Run the check again.
statusCheck.serverLaneDesc: Every cache domain, looked up by the server and checked against your cache's IP.
statusCheck.serverLanePending: Results appear here once the check has completed.
statusCheck.noProblems: No DNS problems found, and nothing looks like it is bypassing the cache.
statusCheck.disabledNote: Skipped: DISABLE_{{service}} is set in lancache-dns, so this service is intentionally not cached.
statusCheck.reasonBlocked: Your DNS deliberately drops this name (0.0.0.0), usually an intentionally blocked telemetry or crash-reporting endpoint. No traffic leaves your network, and downloads are unaffected.
statusCheck.checkDnsWrongIp: {{ip}} is a public internet address, not your cache
statusCheck.checkHeartbeatExpectedMatch: points at your cache's IP, but the cache did not reply this time
statusCheck.checkHeartbeatNone: points into your network, but nothing answered as the cache
statusCheck.checkOriginHttp: answers over plain HTTP, so it can be cached
statusCheck.checkOriginHttpsOnly: looks HTTPS only, so downloads probably skip the cache
statusCheck.checkOriginNoAnswer: did not answer over HTTP or HTTPS
statusCheck.deviceLaneDesc: A quick test from the browser you are using right now. It only speaks for this device.
statusCheck.probeChecking: Checking whether this device reaches the cache…
statusCheck.probeInterceptedDetail: The cache answered this browser (served by {{host}}).
statusCheck.probeInconclusive: Something answered, but the browser cannot confirm it is the cache.
statusCheck.probeInconclusiveDetail: {{domain}} answered this browser, but browser security rules hide the details. Trust the server check above.
statusCheck.probeUnreachable: This device could not reach the cache.
statusCheck.probeUnreachableDetail: {{domain}} did not answer from this device within {{seconds}} seconds.
statusCheck.probeBlocked: Browser probes are blocked on this page.
statusCheck.probeBlockedDetail: This page is served over HTTPS, so the browser refuses plain-HTTP requests to the cache (mixed content). Use the server check above, or open the app over HTTP to test from this device.
statusCheck.probeBlockedFallback: Test from this device in a terminal instead:
statusCheck.probeBlockedFallbackHint: A healthy setup answers nslookup with your cache's LAN IP, and the curl reply includes an X-LanCache-Processed-By header.
statusCheck.testLaneDesc: Look up a single domain and see whether the cache answers.
statusCheck.footerEnvDocker: settings read from the running container
statusCheck.footerNoFetch: NOFETCH is enabled, so the list is never fetched from the network

data.evictedCacheSummary: Find and clean up games nginx evicted from the cache.
data.evictedCacheDescription: Controls how games that have been evicted from the nginx cache are handled. Eviction happens automatically when the cache disk fills up.

evictionModes.showDescription: Evicted game data remains visible on all pages with an 'Evicted' badge and dimmed appearance, included in stat calculations
evictionModes.showCleanDescription: Evicted game data is shown normally without any badge or dimming, included in stat calculations
evictionModes.hideDescription: Evicted game data is hidden from all pages and excluded from all stat calculations
evictionModes.removeDescription: Evicted game data is permanently deleted from the database

data.evictionRemoveConfirmMessage: You are about to switch to Remove mode. Any game data that has been evicted from the cache will be permanently deleted from the database.
data.evictionRemoveConfirmWarning: This action cannot be undone. Deleted records will not be recoverable and will be removed the next time an eviction scan is run.
data.evictionRemoveAllConfirmMessage: This will remove all {{count}} evicted items in a single batched operation (one log rewrite plus database cleanup). The operation cannot be undone.
data.evictionRemoveAllConfirmWarning: Only the evicted depots are removed - partially-cached games keep their on-disk files.
data.evictionRemoveSelectedCancelledWithFailu: Evicted removal cancelled after {{count}} items, with {{failed}} failures
data.gameCacheRemoveAllConfirmMessage: This will permanently delete cache files, log entries, and database records for all {{count}} currently-cached games and services. Items are removed one at a time. The operation cannot be undone.
data.gameCacheRemoveAllConfirmWarning: This is irreversible. Any client that re-downloads these games will have to pull the full payload from upstream, not the cache.
data.evictionScanWaiting: Waiting for eviction scan to finish...
data.pruneOrphanedDownloads: Prune download records with no log history
data.pruneOrphanedDownloadsDescription: During eviction scans, deletes download records only when no log entries remain. Leave this off to preserve statistics for services whose logs were cleared.
data.resetEvictionsSuccess: Reset {{count}} evicted downloads back to normal

corruption.title: Corruption Detection and Removal
corruption.summary: Find structurally invalid cache files or repeated-MISS suspects, then remove only exact paths verified by the saved scan.
corruption.directoryMissing: Removal unavailable: required directories not found
corruption.scanningMessage: Scanning logs for repeated cache misses on files still present on disk… This may take several minutes for large log files.
corruption.scanningRepeatedMissMessage: Scanning logs for repeated cache misses on files still present on disk… This may take several minutes for large log files.
corruption.scanningStructuralMessage: Checking nginx cache headers, payload offsets, and authoritative payload lengths… Only proven structural failures are flagged.
corruption.structuralScanHelp: Full Scan checks every eligible cache file and refreshes the baseline. Incremental Scan checks new or changed files and inspects everything once when no compatible baseline exists.
corruption.scanningStructuralFullMessage: Checking every eligible nginx cache file and refreshing the incremental baseline…
corruption.scanningStructuralIncrementalMessage: Checking new, changed, unresolved, or previously invalid cache files against the incremental baseline…
corruption.buildingStructuralBaselineMessage: No compatible baseline was available. This Incremental Scan is checking every eligible cache file once to create one…
corruption.resumingStructuralIncrementalMessage: Resuming safely committed work from the interrupted Incremental Scan…
corruption.searchPlaceholder: URL, path, reason, or candidate ID…

repeatedMiss.label: Repeated MISS
repeatedMiss.description: Uses repeated successful MISS responses as a heuristic for suspect files still on disk.

structural.label: Structural integrity
structural.description: Finds only proven malformed, truncated, or length-inconsistent nginx cache files.

corruption.sensitivityHighDesc: Requires 3 qualifying MISS observations on one physical slice within 60 seconds.
corruption.sensitivityMediumDesc: Requires 5 qualifying MISS observations on one physical slice within 60 seconds.
corruption.sensitivityLowDesc: Requires 10 qualifying MISS observations on one physical slice within 60 seconds.
corruption.evidenceLookbackDescription: Uses qualifying MISS observations from the {{days}} days before the scan starts.
corruption.coverageSummary: {{checked}} files checked · {{skipped}} safely skipped · {{errors}} I/O errors

empty_cache_file.label: Empty cache file
empty_cache_file.description: The file contains no nginx cache header or payload framing.

truncated_cache_header.label: Truncated cache header
truncated_cache_header.description: The recognized nginx v5 cache header ends before its required fields or cache-key boundary.

malformed_cache_header.label: Malformed cache header
malformed_cache_header.description: The recognized nginx cache header violates a required marker, key, CRC, ETag, or Vary invariant.

invalid_payload_offset.label: Invalid payload offset
invalid_payload_offset.description: The stored payload offset begins before the cached response headers can end.

truncated_before_payload.label: Truncated before payload
truncated_before_payload.description: The file ends before its stored payload offset.

cache_key_path_mismatch.label: Cache key/path mismatch
cache_key_path_mismatch.description: The file path digest matches neither the recorded cache key nor its valid Vary variant.

payload_length_mismatch.label: Payload length mismatch
payload_length_mismatch.description: The stored payload byte count differs from the authoritative Content-Length.

content_range_length_mismatch.label: Content-Range length mismatch
content_range_length_mismatch.description: The stored payload byte count differs from the authoritative Content-Range span.

content_length_range_conflict.label: Length/range conflict
content_length_range_conflict.description: Content-Length and Content-Range declare conflicting authoritative payload sizes.

epicMapping.description: Refreshes the Epic Games Store catalog used to identify Epic downloads in your cache: game titles, CDN URL patterns, and depot mappings.
epicMapping.gain: Newly released or updated Epic games are mapped to their cached downloads sooner, allowing them to appear in the game list without waiting for the next detection cycle
epicMapping.loss: New or renamed Epic games may not appear in the game list until the next scheduled refresh

xboxMapping.description: Refreshes the Xbox / Microsoft Store catalog used to name Xbox downloads in your cache. Reads the owned-games list and per-file CDN patterns from your authenticated Xbox prefill session, with no prefill download required.
xboxMapping.gain: Owned Xbox games are mapped to their cached downloads on a schedule, so titles downloaded on a real Xbox or PC are named without starting a prefill
xboxMapping.loss: Newly acquired Xbox games may not appear in the game list until the next scheduled refresh or a daemon login

logRotation.description: Tells nginx to reopen its access log. Fixes the case where log rotation leaves nginx writing to a deleted file and your stats stop updating.
logRotation.gain: Nginx reliably reattaches to the log file, ensuring download activity is captured correctly
logRotation.loss: If nginx has lost log access, new download activity will not be recorded until the next rotation run

cacheReconciliation.description: Compares your cache directory against the database to see what is still on disk vs. what nginx has evicted. Keeps the 'evicted' badges in the UI accurate.
cacheReconciliation.gain: Eviction badges and cache status in the UI are more accurate and up to date
cacheReconciliation.loss: The UI may show stale eviction status - items marked as cached might actually be evicted, or vice versa
cacheReconciliation.notificationsHelp: Notifications are always shown for manual scans. Enable this to also show them for the automatic scan on startup and the recurring scheduled scan.

cacheSizeScan.description: Walks the entire cache directory and totals every file on disk (mapped and unmapped). Updates the Cache Files dashboard stat, scan timestamp, and unmapped-cache hint.
cacheSizeScan.gain: File counts and unmapped-cache totals stay current without clicking Refresh in Management → Cache
cacheSizeScan.loss: The Cache Files stat and unmapped-cache subtitle may stay outdated until the next scan runs

gameDetection.description: Scans your cache directory and labels each chunk of cached data with the game it belongs to, using the Steam and Epic depot mappings. Incremental scans skip games already detected; previously 'Unknown' depots are resolved as new mappings arrive.
gameDetection.gain: Newly downloaded games appear in the game list sooner, and previously unknown depots are resolved as new mappings arrive
gameDetection.loss: Games downloaded between scans won't show up in the game list until the next detection run

gameImageFetch.description: Downloads the cover art shown next to each detected game. Fills in missing artwork and refreshes any image older than 7 days, trying multiple Steam and Epic CDNs.
gameImageFetch.gain: Game cards get their artwork faster and stale images are kept up to date
gameImageFetch.loss: Some game cards may display without artwork for longer until the next image fetch cycle

steamService.description: Steam caches content by depot ID (a number), not by game name, so it needs a lookup to turn those IDs into games. This service keeps the whole depot-to-game lookup in memory so Steam downloads resolve to game names instantly, caches Steam Store names and cover art on demand, and reloads the lookup after Steam Game Mapping discovers new mappings.
steamService.gain: Depot-to-app lookups stay fast and newly discovered PICS mappings are picked up sooner
steamService.loss: Newly discovered PICS mappings may take longer to appear in depot lookups until the next reload

cacheSnapshot.description: Saves a periodic snapshot of used cache size and total capacity so the dashboard can chart growth over time. Snapshots older than 90 days are deleted automatically.
cacheSnapshot.gain: The cache growth chart has finer granularity, making it easier to spot trends and spikes
cacheSnapshot.loss: The growth chart will have wider gaps between data points, making short-term trends harder to see

operationHistoryCleanup.description: Removes cache-clear operation records older than 24 hours from the history page. Doesn't touch your actual cache files - those are only cleared from the Cache Management page.
operationHistoryCleanup.gain: Completed operation records are automatically cleaned up after 24 hours, keeping the history tidy
operationHistoryCleanup.loss: Operation records older than 24 hours are permanently removed and cannot be reviewed later

performanceOptimization.description: Watches memory use and triggers garbage collection only when it crosses your configured threshold. Also clears stale database connections. Off by default - enable on the Performance Optimizations page if you're memory-constrained.
performanceOptimization.gain: Memory usage stays lower and the UI feels snappier on memory-constrained hosts
performanceOptimization.loss: Brief CPU spikes during each collection pass can cause small pauses in background work

dashboardCacheWarmer.description: Pre-loads dashboard data (active downloads, stats, metrics) in the background so the page opens instantly instead of waiting on a cold database query.
dashboardCacheWarmer.gain: Dashboard opens immediately even after the server has been idle; users never see a cold-fetch spinner
dashboardCacheWarmer.loss: A small amount of background CPU is used each cycle, and cached values may briefly lag real-time changes until the next warm

network.title: Container Network Status
network.publicDnsExpected: Public DNS is expected with host networking. Steam-prefill will detect lancache automatically via localhost. If your cache size grows during downloads, everything is working correctly.
network.publicIpDetected: Public IP detected - lancache-dns may not be configured. Prefill may download from internet instead of populating cache.
network.steamTriggerDomainNotConfigured: This is Steam's official trigger domain. Configure lancache-dns to point this to your cache server IP for optimal performance (32 connections vs 4).
network.primaryDomainNotConfigured: This is the primary CDN domain for this service. Configure lancache-dns to point this to your cache server IP for downloads to be cached.
network.optionalDomainNotConfigured: This domain is optional. As long as the primary domain is configured, prefill will work correctly.
network.hostNetworkingHint: If Steam login hangs or times out, set Prefill__LancacheIp to your lancache server's IP - this fixes most DNS-related issues without changing network mode. As a fallback, try Prefill__NetworkMode=bridge in your docker-compose.yml.
network.lancacheIpResolutionFailed: Prefill__LancacheIp is not set and a lancache server could not be auto-detected. The daemon will fall back to container DNS, which may not resolve every CDN domain (Battle.net/Blizzard CDNs are often missing from lancache DNS) and can cause prefill to hang. Set Prefill__LancacheIp=<your-lancache-server-ip> in your docker-compose for reliable prefill.
network.ipv6UnsupportedHint: IPv6 failures are expected: lancache-dns only supports IPv4. If clients prefer IPv6, disable IPv6 or force IPv4 to avoid bypassing your cache.
network.ipv6BypassDetected: IPv6 resolution detected for this domain. If your router or clients bypass lancache-dns via IPv6, Steam login and prefill can fail. Consider disabling IPv6 or ensuring AAAA records point to your cache.

dataSource.title: Choose Data Source
dataSource.subtitle: Select how to obtain depot mapping data for identifying Steam games
dataSource.depotMappingDesc: Links cache files to games. Essential for identifying which games are being cached.
dataSource.githubDesc: Community-maintained depot mappings. Quick and easy.
dataSource.steamDesc: Generate directly from Steam. Access all games.

steamWebApiKey.howToGet: How to get your API key:
steamWebApiKey.step4: Copy and paste your key below
steamWebApiKey.label: API Key
steamWebApiKey.placeholder: Enter your Steam Web API key
steamWebApiKey.securityNote: Your API key is encrypted and stored securely using Microsoft Data Protection API.
steamWebApiKey.networkError: Network error - failed to test API key

steamPicsAuth.title: Steam PICS Authentication
steamPicsAuth.subtitle: Choose how to authenticate with Steam for depot mapping data
steamPicsAuth.depotMappingDesc: Links cache files to games. Anonymous mode provides public games only. Account login enables access to playtest and restricted games.
steamPicsAuth.accountModeDesc: Access playtest and restricted games via V2
steamPicsAuth.accountModeUnavailable: V2 API unavailable - use V1 API key instead
steamPicsAuth.v2Required: Steam account login requires V2 API which is currently unavailable.
steamPicsAuth.v2RequiredWithV1: Your V1 API key already provides access to playtest/restricted games since it's tied to your Steam account.
steamPicsAuth.v2RequiredNoV1: You can configure a V1 API key later in Settings to access playtest/restricted games.
steamPicsAuth.networkError: Network error - failed to save authentication mode

picsProgress.buildingDesc: Fetching and processing depot information from Steam...
picsProgress.timeEstimate: This typically takes 1-5 minutes depending on your connection.

depotInit.title: Initialize Depot Data
depotInit.subtitle: Choose how to obtain depot mapping data
depotInit.downloadCompleteDesc: GitHub depot data has been imported successfully
depotInit.fetchingDesc: Fetching pre-created depot mappings from GitHub...
depotInit.githubUnavailable: GitHub download unavailable with Steam login. Your personalized depot data will be generated from Steam.
depotInit.failedToDownload: Failed to download pre-created depot data from GitHub
depotInit.unableToStart: Unable to start full scan. Please try again or download from GitHub.

depotMapping.title: Map Game Depots
depotMapping.subtitle: Map depot IDs to game names for your downloads
depotMapping.subtitleComplete: All downloads have been mapped to games
depotMapping.description: This step runs an incremental PICS scan to update depot mappings, then applies them to your downloads.
depotMapping.canSkip: You can skip this and map depots later from the Management tab.
depotMapping.setupComplete: Setup complete! Your Lancache Manager is ready to use.
depotMapping.viewDashboard: View your dashboard for cache statistics
depotMapping.platformsConfigured: Depot mapping will use your configured platform connections to resolve game information.
depotMapping.noPlatformsWarning: No platforms were configured. Depot mapping will use GitHub data only, which may not include the latest game information. You can configure Steam and Epic later in Settings.

logProcessing.title: Process Cache Logs
logProcessing.subtitle: Identify downloads and games from your cache history
logProcessing.subtitleComplete: All cache logs have been processed
logProcessing.subtitleMultiple: {{count}} datasources configured. Import all historical data to identify downloads.
logProcessing.description: Processing imports all log entries from the beginning to identify downloads and games. This can take several minutes depending on log size.
logProcessing.canSkip: You can skip this and process logs later from the Management tab.
logProcessing.complete: Log processing complete! Click Continue to proceed.

importHistorical.title: Import Historical Data
importHistorical.subtitle: Import download history from external database systems
importHistorical.skipNotice: Most users can skip this step. Only use if you have an existing database from a previous installation.
importHistorical.addNewOnly: Only new records will be added.
importHistorical.pleaseEnter: Please enter a database path or connection details
importHistorical.checkLancacheManager: Make sure to select a LancacheManager database for this import type.

platformSetup.title: Platform Setup
platformSetup.subtitle: Choose which gaming platform to configure for game identification

github.label: Download from GitHub (Recommended)
github.description: Quick setup - downloads pre-created Steam depot-to-game mappings from GitHub. No API key or authentication required.
github.note: Best for most users. Data is updated regularly.

steam.label: Steam PICS (Advanced)
steam.description: Generate fresh depot mappings directly from Steam's Package Info Cache System. Provides the most up-to-date data but requires a Steam Web API key and authentication.
steam.note: Recommended for advanced users who need real-time data.

epic.label: Epic Games
epic.description: Connect your Epic Games account to identify Epic game downloads in your cache. Uses OAuth to securely access your Epic Games library.

skip.label: Skip Platform Setup
skip.description: Skip platform configuration for now. You can set up Steam or Epic game mapping anytime from the Management tab.
skip.note: You can always configure this later.

platformSetup.infoBox: Game mapping links cached downloads to specific games. Choose a platform to set up, or skip and configure later.
platformSetup.override: This will replace your current Steam setup

requirementsInfo.label: GitHub only:
requirementsInfo.body: GitHub depot mappings are always available without a login. For the latest Steam and Epic game information (names, icons, and depot details), you'll need to authenticate with those platforms.

platformSetup.softRequirement: Setting up at least one platform is recommended to see game names and icons in your dashboard.

epicAuth.title: Epic Games Authentication
epicAuth.subtitle: Connect your Epic Games account to enable game identification for cached downloads
epicAuth.securityNote: Your Epic credentials are handled securely through Epic's official OAuth flow. No passwords are stored - only a temporary authorization token is used.
epicAuth.enterCodeSubtitle: Complete the login in your browser, then paste the authorization code below
epicAuth.successSubtitle: Your Epic game library will now be used to identify cached downloads
epicAuth.skipNote: You can also set this up later from the Management tab
